mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns. This mechanism reflects the health of the consumer-driven economy.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat during periods of consistent RSAFS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer confidence and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and housing starts are simultaneously declining, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy severely impacting interest-rate sensitive sectors like housing, signaling a broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND HOUST declines by more than 10% year-over-year.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS is high and HOUST is significantly declining.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and often prompts central banks to raise interest rates, increasing the cost of capital for businesses and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with high CPIAUCSL growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) signifies robust manufacturing and mining activity, indicating strong underlying economic demand and productive capacity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth is consistently above 2.0% for 3 consecutive months.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative or flat during periods where INDPRO shows sustained growth above 2.0% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a recessionary environment, leading to widespread declines in corporate profits, increased risk aversion, and a general sell-off in equity markets, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 shows two consecutive quarters of negative growth.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances following two consecutive quarters of negative GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) coupled with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a healthy and growing economy driven by consumer strength, which boosts corporate revenues and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.2%.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of periods where both PAYEMS and RSAFS show strong positive monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low Federal Funds Rate signifies an accommodative monetary policy, which lowers borrowing costs for companies and consumers, encourages investment, and supports higher equity valuations by reducing the discount rate for future cash flows, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently negative during prolonged periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.
